profile.name: Kaluzy
profile.summary: Hold strategy: use the lower credit-bureau score as the conservative scenario, maintain the paid-off car, clear small debt first, and re-check Kia/Tesla offers after a few paychecks.

marketPatterns.pattern: Consumer-first negotiation tools: target prices, invoice data, OTD estimates, junk-fee review, AI/concierge negotiation.
marketPatterns.productLesson: Dream Car Tracker should not just list cars; it should compute a buy/wait signal and tell the user what exact OTD target to demand.

marketPatterns.pattern: Deal pages organize by lease, financing, rebates, monthly payment buckets, fuel type, and expiring incentives.
marketPatterns.productLesson: The dashboard should track APR, lease cash, rebates, expiration date, fuel type, and estimated payment in one card.

marketPatterns.pattern: Car buyer tools combine incentives, prequalification/payment estimates, trade-in appraisal, reviews, and side-by-side comparisons.
marketPatterns.productLesson: A good SaaS version should combine user credit score, trade value, insurance, family requirements, and deal incentives.

marketPatterns.pattern: Monthly 0% APR lists are useful but generic; deals vary by region and require excellent credit.
marketPatterns.productLesson: Personalization is the edge: alert only when a deal matches the user's actual credit/payment/model requirements.

vehicles.dealNotes: Refreshed 2026-06-25. Kia official public offers page is location-gated but confirms current national offer flow; Edmunds/CarsDirect June refreshes show 2026 Sportage/Sportage Hybrid support remains strong for well-qualified buyers. CarsDirect last updated 2026-06-24 lists Sportage financing as low as 0% APR for 48 months with up to $750 finance bonus, up to $3,250 cash back, and meaningful lease cash by trim: LX Hybrid FWD about $1,800, EX Hybrid AWD about $1,150, X-Line Hybrid AWD about $1,200, expiring 2026-07-06. Edmunds shows 0% APR 36/48, 0.9% 60, 2.99% 66, 3.99% 72 for 2026 Sportage gas trims through 2026-07-07; Sportage Hybrid-specific Edmunds text appears stale/region-mixed, so CarsDirect is the cleaner hybrid source. Buy signal stays green only if Kaluzy qualifies and the dealer avoids markup/protection packages.
vehicles.why: Best blend of space, hybrid efficiency, payment realism, and preference fit — but still a watch item until credit/debt position is stronger.

vehicles.dealNotes: Refreshed with Kaluzy's Tesla configurator and conservative credit planning: one consumer bureau scenario is lower while another is higher, so the dashboard uses the lower score as the conservative planning scenario. Tesla showed about 0.99% APR in the configurator before adding trade value, but final auto-lender approval may differ from consumer VantageScore views. A $14k trade scenario could materially reduce payment, but final approval, insurance, and exact trade offer still decide the real fit. Strong APR signal remains, but this is a hold/watch item rather than a buy-now decision.
vehicles.why: Best low-APR EV option; with a strong trade it may fit the payment range, but it stays a hold until trade value, insurance, and final approval are confirmed.

vehicles.dealNotes: Refreshed 2026-06-25. Kia official public offers page is location-gated but active; Edmunds June 2026 Sorento Hybrid incentives show $2,750 customer cash, lease bonus cash examples around $4,050 for 36 months / $4,400 for 48 months plus $375 select-inventory lease bonus, and special APR through 2026-07-07: 0% for 36/48 months, 0.9% for 60, 2.99% for 66, 3.49% for 72, 4.49% for 84. CarsDirect last updated 2026-06-22 lists Sorento lease cash up to $5,600 on PHEV and hybrid-specific lease cash around $3,400 FWD / $2,350 AWD, expiring 2026-07-06, plus 0% APR with up to $750 finance bonus. Rate remains excellent, but this is still a payment stretch because MSRP/OTD is much higher than Sportage.
vehicles.why: Best space, third row for kids, but payment stretch compared with Sportage.
watchTriggers: large lease cash or dealer discount
redFlags: Top trim pushes price too high

vehicles.dealNotes: Refreshed 2026-06-25. Toyota's official RAV4 Hybrid deals page remains local-offer gated and did not expose a specific RAV4 Hybrid APR in extracted public text. Toyota central/dealer offer pages show 2026 RAV4 at 4.99% APR for 60 months through 2026-06-30, and separate RAV4 Plug-in Hybrid examples also show 4.99% APR for 60 months / 5.99% for 72 in some regions. This remains above Kaluzy's 3% red-flag line and far outside the <=2% preference. No meaningful Toyota buy signal unless RAV4/RAV4 Hybrid APR drops below 3%, ideally <=2%, or discounts become large enough to offset the rate.
vehicles.why: Reliable and attractive design, but worse APR and less rear legroom than Sportage.

lastFetchNotes: Refreshed 2026-06-25 using official Tesla/Kia/Toyota public offer pages where extractable plus CarsDirect, Edmunds, Toyota regional/dealer offer pages, and Tesla current-offers coverage; no dealers were contacted and no user data was submitted.

Meaningful change vs 2026-06-22: CarsDirect now shows larger Sportage lease/cash support, including hybrid lease cash around $1,150-$1,800 by target trim plus up to $3,250 total cash back; Sportage remains the best practical buy signal if Kaluzy qualifies.

Tesla Model Y low-APR signal remains active but source snippets conflict on AWD breadth; dashboard keeps the conservative reading of 0% RWD and about 0.99% AWD/Premium until Tesla's official page exposes trim-specific APR in extractable text.
Toyota RAV4/RAV4 Hybrid remains red because public APR evidence is still around 4.99% for 60 months in regional pages, above Kaluzy's 3% red-flag line.
Personal strategy update: maintain the paid-off car, clear small debt first, and avoid turning a good APR into a rushed purchase.
This is a dashboard snapshot, not a dealer-confirmed quote.

APR offers are often for well-qualified buyers and vary by region/trim; public consumer VantageScore views can differ from auto-lender scoring, so the dashboard uses the lower visible bureau score as the conservative planning scenario.
Insurance must be quoted before buying because current private insurance baseline is approximate.
The cron tracker should refresh official/current offer pages twice weekly and only alert on meaningful changes.

tradeValueTracker.apiStatus: No private valuation API key configured. Public site does not expose API keys; licensed valuation APIs should run only in private cron/backend.

trustedSources.name: Kelley Blue Book
trustedSources.apiNotes: KBB/Trade-In Advisor API is B2B/licensed via Cox Automotive/KBB; integrate privately if credentials are available.

trustedSources.name: J.D. Power / NADA
trustedSources.apiNotes: J.D. Power Valuation Services is licensed; use as private backend source if credentials are available.

trustedSources.name: Black Book
trustedSources.apiNotes: Black Book values are enterprise/licensed; strong source for dealer-like trade value if credentials are available.

trustedSources.name: CarMax Instant Offer
trustedSources.apiNotes: No public valuation API for consumer use; manually capture offer or integrate only through approved partner access.

trustedSources.name: Carvana Sell/Trade
trustedSources.apiNotes: Partner API requires authorization; no public consumer trade-in API. Use manually or with approved partner credentials.

latestValues.notes: Current dashboard scenario. Replace with fresh KBB/J.D. Power/Black Book or real cash offers when available.
If offers reach this level, the next-car payment math becomes meaningfully better.

refreshChecklist: Check Black Book if API/license becomes available
Use the lowest credible value for conservative payment planning and the highest real cash offer for negotiation leverage
alertRules: Alert if conservative trade value drops below $13,000
Alert if any real offer is $14,000 or higher
Alert strongly if any real offer is $15,500 or higher
Alert if offer expiration is within 3 days
